title: Chrysaspis patens
taxonomy: Chrysaspis patens is classified as a species within the family Mimosoideae of the flowering plant order Fabales. This plant is further categorized as a member of the genus Chrysaspis within the Fabaceae family. The Chrysaspis patens is a part of the subfamily Mimosoideae, which is a group of plants known for their bipinnate leaves and woody stems. The plant's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Fabaceae family. As a member of this family, Chrysaspis patens shares characteristics such as compound leaves and fruit pods with other Fabaceae species.

Traditional medicine has used Chrysaspis patens to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
